Description: A jagged, wickedly barbed blade of evil, charged with the unreasoning rage of ancestral vendettas.
A peerless triple bow crafted of nightmatter and diamondwire by the exalted bowyers of the primeval overgods.
An otherworldly staff of omnipresent elation, embodied of cardinal essence and the power of deathless peace.
A golden wand of breathtaking power, created by elemental forces from the realms of ice and flame to unleash revenge on their enemies.
A gleaming obsidian sword of imperial power, fashioned by enslaved demons for the personal use of a world-conquering leader of mortals.
An awe-inspiring sword gifted to only the most worthy mortals from the highest heavens, channeling gales of wind in the hands of the blameless.
The weight of a single swing carries the vigor of a hundred past heroes, their souls bound together in the shape of blades so they may once again slay evil.
An ancient longbow forged with an unmatched forgotten technique.
A spellblade forged through the suffering of souls that have lost their way.
A golden morning star of unrelenting power, created for the sole purpose of the destruction.
The primary armament of the gravelords of old, eternally tasked with making sure the dead are forever sealed away from the mortal plane.
The culmination of countless generations of swordsmiths, each perfecting their craft and improving these blades gradually. The pinnacle of weapons created by mortals for use in slaying the immortal.
A moonwoven cloak worn by slayer cults who spill the blood of emperors in their abhorrent mission to create a new order.
A sun iron quiver passed through a royal elven lineage, whose legendary skills in archery have protected the Forest Kingdoms for millennia.
A catastrophic spell of holy destruction, concocted in secrecy by the Liturgical Ones in their quest to learn a single letter of the divine language.
A tome beatified in starlight and written on paper from the ancient wisdom trees, cloaking the worthy in divine reverence.
An ashen helm of regal grandeur, whose wearers have upraised the greatest legions and inspired total domination.
A mighty shield smithed in the burning rivers of the demonic realms to grant the right of sovereignty over the brutal Orclands.
A jubilant seal of celebration, granted to the most righteous crusaders as they challenge the evergrowing threat of demonic legions.
The oceanic Necrofish’s highly noxious poisons, secreted from its spines to befoul entire swaths of the seas.
A horrendously debauched martyr’s skull that has been brought to ruin by foul enchantments of the sulfurous realms.
An explosive ocean trap constructed to tear open the ironhide of the abyssal deepworm.
An anomalous orb utilized by the astralmancers to banish demons who rose against them into a timeless void.
A scintillating prism of duplicity, infiltrating the deepest corners of the mind to awaken phantasmic entities that embody hatred and fear.
An azure scepter of elemental dominion, wielded by the sky charioteers to command the natural order of the overlands and the orchestration of astral bodies.
A heraldic shuriken of the ancient royal family, revered for its astonishingly lethal edge after generations of use by a mighty eastern empire.
A pristine wakizashi exalted by the archangels themselves and awarded to combatants with the most pure hearts.
A mysterious lute of dark matter, capable of playing joyous songs even in the oppressive sound void of space.
A supremely dominant mace of faultless design, capable of manifesting entire armies in the astral wars.
A marvelous sheath of inimitably brilliant constitution. Drawing one’s blade from within emits a furious sonic wave, propelling the weapon beyond its known limits.

A gilded sigil with ruby gemstones, delicately shaped into the form of the Rhino. This form represents the pinnacle of normal druids, only surpassed by druids choosing to specialize in more mythical creatures.
Magnificent leather armor made from the hide of the great leviathan, slain hundreds of years ago in a savage battle in the vast deeps.
A phenomenal robe magically woven from fibers of sapphire, topaz and lapis lazuli, created for the archwizard of the seraphic realms.
Impenetrable forceplate armor worn by the blackguard conquerors of the necromantic uprising.
An impeccably pristine topaz embedded in an enchanted gold ring.
An impeccably pristine amethyst embedded in an enchanted gold ring.
An impeccably pristine spinel embedded in an enchanted gold ring.
An impeccably pristine onyx embedded in an enchanted gold ring.
An impeccably pristine emerald embedded in an enchanted gold ring.
An impeccably pristine citrine embedded in an enchanted gold ring.
An impeccably pristine ruby embedded in an enchanted gold ring.
An impeccably pristine sapphire embedded in an enchanted gold ring.
A swift and hungry blade that is never satisfied with just one kill.
Archdemon Malphas took a conspicuous liking to this outsider’s sheath, smuggled in by a newly demonic soul. Perhaps its design reminded him of something?
This armor is donned by the Brutes of the Abyss, focusing narrowly on pure offensive power with little reguard to the wellbeing of its wearer.
In the primal era of the realm, the volcanic mountains were overrun with rampant lava spirits, spiteful creatures who sought to burn all they could touch.
A young enchantress used a rare flame-absorbing stone to capture the lava spirits.
A suffocating black smoke began to pour out, consuming the young enchantress. As her soul and mind burned to dust, the fibers of this robe were strangely unscathed.

With the enchantress reduced to ash, the stone burst, scattering the relics of the mystic into the realm’s lava fissures. They have since awaited a daring hero to recover the pieces from the clutches of demons.
A sword that, when held by one true of heart, will release magic from the flawless ruby in its hilt and ignite in flames.
A masterpiece of craftsmanship, this full helm was forged in the fires of the volcanic abyss infusing its metal with a regenerative ability.
This sword’s blade is infused with magical essence, causing it to fire out violently upon lunging. That same magical force pulls the blade back to its hilt after striking.
Decorated with ancient amber, this helmet’s idle solitude over many years have caused its abilities to dwindle.

Created to guide the spirits of the desert, this seal has infused itself with their energy over many years. This spiritual energy affects living creatures in unconventional ways compared to normal practices.
I courageously descended into the ruins, only to be bombarded with cactus spines as sharp as arrows.
After fending off men made of clay, I discovered a lamp that might fetch a considerable sum at the market.
To my shock, a spirit arose from the lamp and thanked me for freeing him. The Genie would only allow me access to the contents of the lamp if I proved myself against his vast spiritual power.
Though his magic was strong, my resolve was stronger. The spirit dissipated and all that remained was the treasure within.
A high-fructose ring that will give you pep, but hurt your concentration.
Reinforced with magical hard candy from the candy forest. Sticky but strong.
No one would dare question your dominance of the Hunting Grounds while wearing the remains of a decapitated gumball machine.
Pixies are often a more aggressive fae. It makes great sense that they would provide heroes with swords as fine as this.
Protect the helpless, embolden the heroic, slow the progress of evil. - Inscription, in Fae.
This colorful platemail is enchanted with special protections, a gift from the kind fairies that dwell in the Realm.
Many do not realize how powerful a kind wish is.
A youthful jellyfish taken from the reefs of the ocean realms, residing within a coralglass jar.
An intricate scepter of unfathomable power containing the electric fluids of a jellyfish behemoth.
The fungus covering this armor has eaten away all the metal. While offering little protection, its restorative powers make this armor unique.

The worms of the Crystal Caverns are capable of surviving indefinitely so long as their heads remain intact. The affixation of this particular worm’s head onto a quarterstaff prevents it from regenerating the rest of its body and causes it to angrily lash out, biting anything brought near it.

An ornately decorated buckler made from an array of crystalline stone. It is too frail to swing around, but it can somehow grow and mantle its wielder in a heavy crystal armor that releases shards upon recieving trauma.
An ancient druidic spell that invokes earth spirits into furnishing the soil with a noxious, deadly skullshroom colony.
A long-lost druid’s tome that catalogs medicinal mushrooms. It also has many pocket spaces that contain spore samples, which can be made fully grown through incantations.
A ceremonial trap used by the elite members of the Mushroom Tribes to ensnare those who trespass their sacred sanctum.
Cut from a fallen star, a deep purple emanating an unearthly glow, hinting at the power fused within.

An incredibly complex and durable crystalline structure appears on this pendant where a druid might expect the representation of an animal. Familiarising oneself with its forms is incredibly difficult but druids who succeed find themselves to be unbreakable walls against all forms of threat.
Though its composition is merely a sharp crystal embedded in a rock, this naturally formed blade glistens with light, carving a path for its wielder to use.
These kunai were chiseled as an experimental weapon by the mineral monsters lurking far beneath the realm’s surface.
A devastating fracture in the center of this breathtaking armor tarnished its raw defensive abilities, but the dubious blessing of the Crystal Entity still allows it to aid the wearer in other ways.
What serves as the life support for all of the artificial creatures constructed by the Crystal Entity is also capable of bestowing strength and energy to organic life.
Believed to be some kind of artificial organ, this sheath embeds the weapon in a solid crystal coating before violently fracturing into shrapnel when drawn.
An impossible intracate pattern belies these blades, carved from a self-perfecting crystalline deity.
This heavy cloak cleverly conceals its user not by magic, but by channeling the light around them as camouflage. Tampering with this light briefly projects a false mirror image of oneself.
A fragmented prism that is constantly mending its cracks and refracturing. When agitated, it ejects volatile shards towards the source until each fragment spirals back into equilibrium.
Carved from the unblemished minerals deep beneath the realm, this unconventional wakizashi fractures itself when swung to release a strange supernatural energy.
A perfectly cut crystal encasing light from the oldest stars in the universe. All who hold it are filled with a sense of peace and concentration.
This strange trinket creates a cloud of crystaline particles rendering anyone inside invisible.
Legend has it that this wand was written into existence by a book that wrote itself.
A tattered tome possessing the forbidden knowledge of pagan priests of old. It speaks of casting terrible curses upon opponents, but such impure power comes at a cost.
A symbolic seal gifted to the most renowned philosophers of ancient times.
An artifact once wielded by a wandering samurai with no master to serve. It was preserved in the Cursed Library for historical purposes, but it remains just as deadly despite its age.
A fragment of Lord Ruthven’s curse manifest itself into this pulsating dagger. Its formless state allows it to cut in ways no other weapon could.
Distinguished archers are permitted to take their prized bow with them to the afterlife. It conjures ethereal arrows when drawn, penetrating the very souls of living beings.
A quiver not meant for the corporeal realm. When used by a mortal, it creates a momentary rift between death and life, briefly releasing the chilling screams of the lost.
An enduring garment of spectral steel worn by the shades of the shadows.
An otherworldly gemstone is set in this oddly tight ring. It does not seem designed for human fingers.

A foreign sheath stolen from unknown lands by the crew of Davy Jones. Now phantoms, the headstrong ghost pirates have misused this delicate instrument as a rapier holder, twisting its effects with their chaotic ways.
A dagger that was condemned to Davy Jones’ Locker.
A cursed piece of Davy Jones’ treasure. Using it leaves behind a deadly spirit bomb.
A ghostly fabric from the other side.
Captain Davy Jones’ ring.
A treasure from Davy Jones’ Locker.
A treasure from Davy Jones’ Locker.
A treasure from Davy Jones’ Locker.
Used only by the pirates who would make themselves kings, this cutlass is said to be cursed as each Pirate King seems to be killed rather unexpectedly.
A rather peculiar instrument played by the sailors of the seven seas. Its music has the unique property of attracting parrots that will obey the musician’s every command.
A clumsy yet vicious weapon favored by marauders from coast to coast. Only an experienced pirate king can perform the techniques required to unlock its full potential.
Pirate captains wear extravagant cocked hats to both inspire their men and intimidate their foes.
The buccaneers that raided the realm before Oryx’s rule famously wore such attire. Its opulence does not make for the most practical choice when sailing, but it was valued as a status symbol.
Less prominent pirates use personal hooks to signify their rank. Though this one is almost entirely symbolic on its own, no true pirate is complete without one.
A crude walking stick enchanted by Skuld’s distinct style of sorcery, constructed to guide fallen spirits to the afterlife.
A foul poison harvested from the corpses of plague victims. It infects many targets, but kills slowly.
A worn and battered suit of armor embedded with a mystic gem that flows with magical strength.
A historical amulet that has been passed down for generations. Although once cursed in the past, it still holds a hint of its ancient power.
Made of a strange metal not known to the living. Its connection to the spirit plane causes a high pitched shriek as it slashes.
Woven from the skin of a ghast, Skuld’s ability to freely shift between the mortal and spiritual plane is linked to this cloak’s unusual fabric.
With little need for conventional armor, Skuld prefers to empower her magic arts even further.
The charm embedded into this ring grants the power to absorb and redirect souls for placement in the afterlife.
The arrows of this grotesque bow are laced with an inverted energy, causing shots to lose velocity and create brief barriers.
Dr. Terrible modeled this staff in the likeness of his creation as an experimental attachment to the M.E.R.V. design. To prevent overheating, the unstable energy discharges require a moment to cool.
Firing from this quiver uniquely applies the force of the bowstring as potential energy, which is then overcharged and converted to kinetic energy for a delayed, yet intensly powerful release.
This lightwave blade is carefully attuned to madness harmonics in a revolutionary attempt to remotely blast any region with the fury of the F.E.R.A.L.’s energy attack.
The gouged eye of the ferocious F.E.R.A.L. It is no longer potent enough to generate energy beams, but the beast’s psychic vision still instinctively lashes back at aggressors.
A highly versatile tool modified from what was once just a vial holder. It can extend and retract, tighten for wrench functions, and shoot energy blasts. All the usual tools for science!
Development of this compact M.E.R.V. took an explosive turn.
Not to be confused with a more traditional lab coat, this robe is steeped in chemicals to protect from radiation. This same substance surrounds the labratory’s protective A.E.G.I.S. system.
This feat of technology enables approved users to control all primary functions of the lab facility and access otherwise encrypted data.
Perhaps all that remains of Esben, whoever he was. Sprite Credits: Pfiffel
The skull still seems to contain some of the Void Entity’s power.
A hardy slab of ice wounded with crude markings on its surface. Passionate wedding vows are faintly readable.
A fragmented prism that is constantly mending its cracks and refracturing. Pulling pieces apart can create an unstable link between two spaces as the prism tries to become whole again.
A memento of a life long gone. You can still feel the warmth from years past through the frigid ice that has taken over.
The frostbitten winds whisper her name, and the blades sing of her sorrow.

A glacial robe worn by the witch doctors of a bygone ice tribe during their sacred burial ceremonies. The passage of time has encased the cloth in a layer of permafrost, chilling those who fracture its coat.

Elite Viking warriors and guards were given these great battle axes to use in combat. The mighty Dalvar, strongest of all the Vikings, was known to cut through enemy defenses with this finely balanced weapon.

Recovered from the wreckage of a Viking ship, this large buckler can strike unlike any other shield. It can sustain the force of its strike in the air itself, uniquely constructed to mimic the Viking tale of Thielief the Steadfast catching a relic suspended in the air during a raid of saintly territory.

Painstakingly forged through an immeasurable amount of iron rings linked together, this armor allows its wearer to remain more agile and flexible than traditional armors. The speed rivals that of the legendary Viking Diggsun, whose tide-turning charge on the edge of the battlefield won a great war.
A ceremonial horn gifted to the Vikings from their ancient gods. Blowing it is said to temporarily bestow to the owner the power of Odin, known by some northern clans as Zimmer.
The harbinger will tread through the Tundra, uncaring of the life that may have ended at the hands of the deepsteel blade.
To whom this symbol belonged, and what it may have represented, remains a mystery. It serves as a grim reminder of the world unobserved, cloaked in the endless snow.
The armor from the pitiful resistance was absorbed into this endless deepsteel plate. As the blizzard rages on, the plates grow ever more dense.
In the white wastes, Squall answers no prayers, spares no lives, and grants no solace.
The harbinger will tread through the Tundra, uncaring of the life that may have ended at the hands of the deepsteel blade.

The Harbinger’s power was used to sublimate icicles along this blade, sending many sharp projectiles towards potential foes. He would soon learn that this was useful against more than just those who would oppose the Eternal Frost.
The suit, capable of withstanding the great cold, represented a pact between a mortal and an ever-living elemental being. Humans however, live too short lives to keep promises.
Perhaps at some point, a man wore this pendant in search of something that humans could possibly offer. As the pendant lost meaning, it became a reminder that what he sought was not of mortal nature.

Placing this trinket into an appropriate slot found on Steamworks machines activates their overclock drastically increasing output. The increased speed causes a greater amount of wear on the parts of the machine, usage of this item is only authorized to higher ranking Kogbolds.

Efforts by lower workers of the kogbolds to arm themselves against invaders has resulted in various interesting crudely made armaments. Fashioned from the boiler of a steam generator, this staff projects its scalding vapors towards a target, cooking them alive.

A large sheet of plating intended to be used as a blast shield in explosives testing. When confronted with intruders some of the more cowardly Kogbolds choose to use them to protect themselves while fleeing.
Of all the tools found on Kogbold excavation bots, none is more versatile than the KI225 Chain Cutter. Useful for cutting through unwanted obstructions ranging anything from stone to flesh.

Early prototype versions of the KI470 Laser pointing device came with no inbuilt limiter on their power output. First batch testing resulted in many schematics cut clean in half, as well as multiple layers of steel wall.

This electrical rod is actually used to remove rust from machine parts through electrolysis. When used on a living or artificial creature however, the shock causes muscles to tense and circuits to go haywire, reducing the assailant’s power.

The oldest forms of automatic computing built by the Kogbolds were a far cry from the self improving artificial intelligence of the modern Core designs. Though its computation capabilities are lost to time, the matrix remains conveniently effective at distributing powerful surges of energy.
A well machined object said to give the Kogbolds their strength.
The KI132 Bladed Multitool finds its way into the hands of many Kogbold Engineers sent out on expeditions. It is equipped with blades for any situation, as well as a handy pair of pliers.
An ancient weapon imbued with the power of the cosmos.
Light armor crafted from the hard-as-steel leafy scales of a Leaf Dragon.
A magic robe woven from enchanted threads of crystalline silk torn out of the water harvested from a great Water Dragon’s body.
Battle armor made from Fire Dragon scales, one of the strongest substances this world has ever seen.

Legends speak of a star touched by Horizon that pierces through the light of the heavens. As eons passed, the caged tyrant sharpened broken scales like razorblades, imbuing his hatred as inky black darkness to forge a dark myth into reality.
As the heavens decreed, the age of dragons came swiftly to an end. Yet the gaze of the mighty watch intensely over the world, waiting for the power of Draconis to rise once again.
This relic was once used by a secluded cult of dragon worshippers. A druid may tap into its arcane power to embrace the form of a dragon.
Tugging the bowstring fiber disrupts a delicate balance between the limbs of this bow, rapidly crystallizing the surrounding frost into a serrated bolt of hail.
Baneful beats of this waist-worn instrument rile the skies, invoking the occluded power of the storm.

Hardly attire of any sort, but rather a sustained cone of latent energy called forth by unseen wind spirits. Stray gusts glide beneath each step, affording both impressive speed and a thrilling lighter-than-air sensation.
A breathtaking amulet believed to portend the will of the storm. The large stone within faintly swirls, as though an entire cyclone were somehow petrified by force of the earth.

Strictly speaking, this is no wand at all, and rather blunt even for a channeling device. Yet its crude appearance belies a sophisticated tool of deterrence, a contrast very much in character for the spirits of the earth.

To fabricate colossi from within the earth is a rare and enviable practice, often deemed sacred. The creations of this club-like mace are too brittle for the interest of certain parties, but the proven potential is a precious thing indeed.
The practices of the earth mages died out with their bloodline, a consequence of notoriously being rather secretive types. But the traits of this garb, hardy and unyielding, tell much of their ways.

When etched into stone, this mark greatly amplifies one’s resilience, as though it echoes the earth’s power. Hollow relics like these are an oddity, as earth shamans regarded the designed permission of flowing air as a sign of severe fault.
In the right hands, this fluid blade lashes out with stinging water whips. Such adept control over the wild waters is thought to be a sign of great achievement for any swordsman.
Masters of swordsmanship recognize how much can be learned from the flow of the ocean’s waves.
Reinforced coral creates a form of shock absorbing plates, a crucial trait for surviving the high pressure of the ocean floor.
A keepsake held closely by Thessal, clinging to the unlikely chance of her king’s continued wellbeing. Any sailor knows this slim hope is all that contains the true beast of the seas.
All initiates who start the path of becoming a Dragon Tamer are given a special weapon. If an initiate completes the Path alive, this weapon is given a name by the Elders.
The last piece of the Taming ritual requires a man to be bound to a Dragon. Wizards were able to link a man to a Dragon’s spirit using magic and pain. Using its powers comes with a great cost.
Made from the Heart of Zaarvox by Ulricht the first Dragon Tamer.
Given to the initiates who survive the first challenge.
A blade created within the Hellforge and enchanted with dark magics to grant its wielder the power of a demon king.
This skull glows with the stolen soul of a phoenix trapped in the skull many years ago by Shaitan.

It is believed that astral stardust landed during the Primal Era, giving life to these now-extinct flowers that adapted to any environment they bloomed from. Consecrated by the infernal spirits of old, these artifacts now serve as a token of friendship with the elementals of the deep earth.
A light powerful blade, capable of burning even the toughest opponents.
This sheath can be a great tool in the hands of an experienced Kensei.
Made of refractory material, this heavy armor is capable of summoning a powerful creature for extra defense.
A beautiful accessory which burns constantly to guide you through battle.
A sword with an impossibly sharp edge. It has been designed with holy magic to slash like no other blade.
A seal made from the Marble Colossus’ head. The power of a pure and mighty paladin still remains within.
A fine sheet of marble charged with the same energy that powers the Marble Colossus. What it lacks in physical durability, it makes up for with a life-breathing aura.
The source of power for the Marble Colossus found within his core. It is intended to give life to golems, but it can provide benefits to the living as well.
A makeshift dagger hastily crafted from the debris of a fallen golem. It may be blunt, but it is also very lightweight.
A fragment of a brain from a lesser golem. Its strength indicates it came from a Golem of Anger.
Formed of the tissue surrounding a golem core.
Used to bind aggressive golems. Considering they are no longer attached, they do not appear to have been very effective.
Jagged obsidian and smooth malachite blades inscribed with runes and teeming with energy. The runes read as a eulogy for those lost while trying to seal a great evil.
A commanding staff used by vile cult leaders. Its power manifested through countless sacrifices to the demon Molek.
The cursed skull of a corrupted mind. Good intentions can go horribly wrong.
A thin hood with a faded golden lace worn for sacrificial ceremonies. It has an aura of evil around it.
A seemingly shattered ring that keeps itself together with an unknown magnetic force. It only stays together on the finger of a true killer.
An oddly heavy vial found within the undergrounds of the Lost Halls. It is said to contain the embodiment of Evil itself.
Every shot that hits a target frees the soul of a forgotten hero. Every shot that misses is trapped for eternity.
A mysterious quiver formed of pure evil and dark matter. Its ethereal existence creates a small implosion of nearby atmosphere when drawn.
This armor does not deflect projectiles like most materials known to man. It absorbs and engulfs them, sending them to a new plane of reality.
Unknown.
An unfathomable amount of strength pulses through this ring. Only the most righteous mortals can touch this ring and live.
This wand is powerful at conducting magical energy into a devastating blast.
This scepter’s blast is so powerful it will slow opponents.
A well-worn robe used for Science!
An early prototype ring.
Although destroyed, the beast’s brute strength remains deeply embedded in this odd wand. Its rage possesses you, and lends you its force.
A scepter that can be evoked to unleash overwhelmingly powerful blasts upon others. Even the most crazed scientists wouldn’t use this scepter without taking note of its side effects.
A rigid hide, chemically engineered by Dr. Terrible and reinforced with rough scales. Though torn apart during battle, it remains powerful.
Fetid claws imbued with dangerous chemicals, pulled from the Horrific Creation.
A treasure from the Mad Lab.
A treasure from the Mad Lab.
The souls of legendary heroes live on in this vibrant elderwood staff. It represents the ancient druidic pact to protect the domains of Fey across the Realm.
The crystalline glass in this orb seals away an ancient flora said to rejuvenate the spirit and unleash one’s force of will against those who threaten the wild.
The evergreen plant fibers woven into this robe pulse with an unusual vibrance. Consecrated in the magic of the forest, this garment lifts your steps and emboldens your mind.
The magical drop of resin fixed atop this ring is a precious keepsake of the Fey. Those who possess it are protected by an old pact formed by the keepers of the forest.
This shimmering shield allows knights to enhance their concentration on targets, giving them more time to think about their next move in battle.
Decorated with the finest flora of the realm’s toughest forests, this bow uses the sharp cymes of plant life as arrows against would-be vandalizers.
Sprouted and plucked from an ancient, enchanted riverbank, this flower’s spores weaken the roots of evil and strengthen righteous hearts.
A lightweight floral coat fitted with the molted shell of a colossal insect. The energy of its evolution still pulses through, reacting upon blows.
Expertly carved from ancient trees and fitted together with their sap, this ring’s flowers have absorbed magical energy over many years, storing it within its fruit.
A dagger made of razor-sharp bone.
Ruthven hid this blade in the hope none would be able to find it, for it could destroy him and his spawn in a single slash.
A wand made from a brilliant white cross designed to ward off evil.
An ancient tome cloaked in white leather, created to aid in the struggle against the ultimate evil.
An ornate robe worn by servants of the faith.
A ring that symbolizes divine might.
A two-faced blade enchanted with the wicked power of immortal beings.
A dark and flowing cape donned by masters of the twilight hours.
The hide of a false creature given sentience by profane life-twisting magic, forever cursed as an affront to the laws of mortal life.
A ruby-beaded necklace, carried by the cadaverous followers of Lord Ruthven.
A treasure from the Manor of the Immortals.
A treasure from the Manor of the Immortals.
A worn plate from the Sage’s armor. Its outwardly poor condition belies a strange, revitalizing effect still flowing within.
A festival coat woven from thin, spiritual threads. It allows the user to skirt the spirit realm in search of their destination.
A beautiful set of ornate ceremonial robes, filled with the energy benevolent spirits from long ago.
A sodden artifact held by Genji to stir nearby moisture into a torrent of localized rain. Only the Sage can extract its full potential, gracefully guiding the morning dew into a lasting downpour.
The drum whose every beat carries with it the energy and rhythm of Kaguya. A mortal sounding this mystical instrument bears both order and disarray.
The ornate umbrella carried by the gracious Miko. It never goes out of fashion, regardless of the season.
A bow believed to have only been fired in dissent against the heavens. In retribution, the empyrean rulers have cursed this device to tether its wielders in shackles of divine light.
These fireworks are a mere fraction of Umi’s spectacular stockpile. Locals believed they held the power to ward off evil spirits, whilst bringing peace and prosperity to its celebrants.
An ethereal lantern charged with the hundreds of hopes and memories of bygone souls. Its light seeks to guide and protect those still dwelling within the mortal plane.
Pure soul energy that burns bright in the fires of the forge.
A fishing rod used by master fishers. It requires skill and precision to use to its full potential.
A fishing rod with a steel alloy frame. Its light weight allows for faster casting.
A sturdy fishing rod reinforced with metal. It is an adequete tool for any fishing activities.
Contained within the crate is the embodiment of the emotion one experiences when unboxing something exciting.
Made from a cherry tree branch. This wand was gifted by Xil to Daichi for bringing him into this world.
This Orb creates a concentrated area of energy. The energy is so dense at its core that foes are paralyzed.
A grand scroll capable of summoning sharpened emerald shards, slicing through even the most durable opponent.
Still believing himself a monk, Daichi knew an open blade contrasts his superficial rank. Yet he could not bear to abandon his wicked material weapon, and so he forever concealed it with this sheath.
A Katana made to harness the energy of the Wind.
Calls upon old energy to unleash a swarm of dragon like energy spirits.
While being made of soft fabric, this outfit is mysteriously sturdy.
The first and last teardrop of the ominous fiery demon.
A bow fashioned from living coral found only in the deepest ocean trenches. It is the favored weapon of those that inhabit the realms beneath the waves.
A trap imbued with deadly venom from the coral of the Ocean Trench.
Mermaid-made armor woven from unbreakable still living coral threads.
A ring crafted from the dangerous Fire Coral.
This necklace is adorned with the tooth of a great white shark. Druids who familiarize themselves with this item may take on its form becoming the apex of the seas.
A decorated forked weapon which can influence and control water, sending torrential downpours of calamity at aggressors.
An enchanted parchment, written in the Slurpian dialect. Its magic commands the tides, allowing one to propel their own movement.
Woven together from the silk of the oceanic Deepstriders, this robe is completely resistant to the tides of the ocean. Such garments are commonplace for royal Slurp attire.
As a memento of their kingdom, royal slurps are given one of these sacred pearls when adventuring out into the world.
A slow but powerful sword that is a relic of a long forgotten age.
A shield from a place beyond the gates of war and peace.
A pitch black cloak enhanced by the defensive strength of Oryx’s own signature armor.
Oryx’s so-called priests use cruel wands like this to inflict suffering on their recipients. They themselves must endure this pain to begin the path to consecration.
Those who wish to be hallowed among Oryx’s legion are required to drink the wretched concoction within this chalice, a mix between the Mad God’s poorly kept wine and his own sentient bile.
Knowing they are to serve a more aggressive purpose on the front lines, Oryx’s chosen messengers utilize a plated robe, complete with a crimson cape.
When the arduous path to anointment is complete, the priest is decorated with this symbolic ring of servitude, modeled after Oryx’s famed warriors.
This tome was carried to battle by the members of a forgotten order.
Forged eons ago in the fires of Shaitan’s domain, this infamous blade has cut down countless mighty heroes throughout the ages.

This almighty shield was specially designed to protect its owner from a bombardment of attacks in all directions. Although feared today, it once stood as a reminder of Oryx’s glory in an era long forgotten.

Skuld enchanted this signature armor by channeling Oryx’s relentless rage into its metal plates. This aura of anger now grants the strength of unbridled fury to anyone reckless enough to wear it themselves.
It is believed that Oryx siphons a drop of blood from each hero he slays for use in this intimidating crimson ring.
This aptly named staff is the manifestation of Chancellor Dammah’s assertions of grandeur. Although he seldom uses it for actual combat purposes, it is no less potent as a legitimate weapon.

Dammah’s powers of summoning portals stem from this profane spell. Most may use it to call attacks from primitive dimensions of untapped energy, but Oryx’s greatest thaumaturges can create stable gateways to the realm and beyond.
The diplomacy of Chancellor Dammah goes as far as his limited patience. The true purpose of this robe is its deeply woven enchantment to bolster arcane powers.
The warped mind of Oryx’s appointed chancellor. This cursed object thrives in the presence of demented energies.
The pride of Treasurer Gemsbok, this gilded knife has backstabbed countless traders in shady dealings.
A coin pilfered from the endless vault of Gemsbok’s hoarded wealth. Only the most daring would stake their lives on the equal odds of a coin flip.
This covering is assigned to Oryx’s negotiators to conceal weaponry and get away with stolen fortunes.
Treasurer Gemsbok had this monocle custom made to enhance his perception in stealth strikes.
This ceremonial mace controls the forces of light and darkness. Archbishop Leucoryx uses it for distinguished offerings in the name of his god.
The sacred writings of Leucoryx and his disciples, compiled as a tribute to Oryx to lend false credence to the teachings of his twisted followers.
This cursed robe overwhelms the wearer with conflicting thoughts of good and evil, shattering the will of the weak-minded.
A majestic crown of the venerated, bestowed upon Archbishop Leucoryx to solidify his position in Oryx’s legion.
Chief Beisa designed and forged this sawtoothed blade himself to be granted admission as a gladiator. Its shattering strikes are amplified by his own slashing techniques.
To dominate the battlefield, Beisa’s assault units possess this star model to remain vigorous in combat before controlling zones with its independent throwing arcs.
Specially weighted plates layered inside this armor channel greater force into every motion of the body for destructive blows.
The battle standard of Beisa’s forces, a sign of triumph for Oryx and calamity for those he faces.
The chosen blade of Oryx the Mad God, charged with an unrelenting energy to subdue all that lies before it.
An enormous shield decorated with the crest of Oryx, an infamous symbol of oppression dreaded across the realm.
Although refurbished, this breastplate is the very same Oryx received as a gift from the royal family in celebration of his gladiatorial prowess.
A horn claimed from Oryx’s iconic helmet. To hold this in one’s hand is a monumental insult to the Mad God’s ego.
A delightful instrument played among the serene choirs of saintly singers in the hallowed realms above.
A resplendent trumpet used to signify the coming of the end times, prophesied to sound their most glorious note at the opening of the seventh seal.
A breathtaking pair of delicate white wings used by the empyrean seraphim to reach the highest peaks of the heavens.
A ring of light bestowed upon the virtuous as a divine symbol of their consecration.
Deep within the blood, cartilage, and twisted bone lies the cranium of a once-human victim.
Believed to have been lost in a bygone era, this spell was recovered from ruins infested by horrific creatures of unknown origin. The scroll is still steeped with parasitic organisms.
A loathsome scepter that converges the life energy of parasites into a soul-rotting bolt of destruction.
An ancient tome covered with fleshy appendages. It contains the secrets to magic often considered blasphemous by priests, used to inflict horrible pain on others.
The melding of these creatures and their host is one of pain and suffering.
Swarming cells fill this jar, prepared to bind to whatever may foolishly approach them.
The parasites wait until the most opportune time, then strike at their targets. They become hosts to a greater evil.
The parasites are not individual beings; they share a single consciousness.
The perfect tool for dismembering theatre equipment.
This prism has a deep flaw that causes it to scatter light in two directions.
A festive piece of armor, complete with bells. Try not to get too crazy.
The classic kit of a magician, built to both dazzle and destroy one’s enemies.
This enchanted cane adds pizzazz to any battle! The cleverly engineered lighting effects inside become stronger for a bigger audience.
Filled with all the necessary tools of showmanship, this dapper robe is a must-have for any true magician.
A classy top hat known to have all manner of surprises in store, including rabbits, of course.
A deadly repeater crossbow and a marvel of craftsmanship. This mechanical weapon is efficient, but carries additional weight.
Seemingly bleak at first, this prism has erratic magical properties looming inside, which affect the light it scatters in unforeseen ways.
The Tlatoani used this robe to aid them in casting their most powerful magic spells. Like their other ceremonial garbs, it rarely saw much combat application.
Adult male basilisks are apex predators, and at full maturity their tails gain the unique ability to release piercing acid from the tip.
An undamaged version of a well known archaeological artifact, radiating a potent dark energy. This rare find is coveted by many explorers.
This spell’s destructive power is so notorious that casting it was banned for many generations.
First summer storm cloud Inflicting a thousand cuts Brilliance, then ash.
Circle of sunlight Empowers a fragile bloom And scours the world.
Scarf held to the breeze Commanding a storm’s vengeance Hoping for silence.
Spiraling treasure Prayer beads for the downpour Coiled in grateful hands.
A shield made of snake skin stretched across a rigid frame.
This strange weapon was used by ancient battle mages to deter invaders. The secret to its construction has long been lost.
A ceremonial pungi that contains the spirits of fifteen snakes, guiding the righteous through their many paths ahead.
A tight fitting garment of snake skin that is both functional and stylish.
A ring made from the eye of the Snake Queen.
Two of Groundskeeper Gretch’s most coveted tools, tainted by a powerful curse that draws out the life essence of its user to maintain their finesse.
A tattered piece of cloth crudely fashioned into a robe, barely capable of covering its wearer. However, what it lacks in physical durability is made up with a thick barrier of mana.
A chilling metal container filled with miscellaneous medical instruments coated in poison. Its syringes require blood as a catalyst, taking a considerable toll on both victim and user alike.

A tightly-woven garment used to restrain the mentally insane in the Penitentiary’s torture branch. The echoed laughs of its past users can be faintly heard while wearing it, and intensify as its wearer takes damage.
The incredibly thick cornea taken from the eye of Overseer Oculon. It bears an immense hunger for magical energy, and will devour its user’s entire mana pool in the blink of an eye.
A questionably sentient artifact smuggled from the Penitentiary’s administration branch. Repeatedly irritating the summoned entity causes it to spew corrosive tears in a large area.

An extremely heavy axehead salvaged from Griefkeeper Zole’s rather bulky choice of weaponry. Throwing it takes a tremendous amount of effort, but it has the capacity to obliterate everything in its path.

A faintly magical device used by Griefkeeper Zole to amplify his own voice throughout the Penitentiary’s cell branch. Peculiarly, speaking into it warps any attempts at encouragement into toxic insults.
An uncannily heavy iron ball and chain utilized by Murcian himself, burdened by the weight of countless souls that met their fate at the hands of the warden.
A luminescent vial containing an eerie liquid, extracted through the complete pulverization of a soul.
Once the most powerful staff in existence; a crack in its frame has rendered it incapable of focusing fire. It remains uniquely deadly.
An enchanted cloak crafted by an ancient guild of timeshifter thieves.
Limon’s power of nature restoration are infused into this mace for her children to wield, traversing the realm and planting seeds of new life in the wake of wanton destruction.
A wand created from the wood of Sprite trees. Seems to be poorly calibrated, though.
A peculiarly weathered rock with runes burned into it from generations of being submurged in the acidic bog. This makeshift snare is unmatched in power, but it activates on its own terms.
This ornate herding staff composed of an old willow branch has been warped beyond mortal comprehension due to prolonged influence of the twisted beings of the Sulfurous Wetlands.

A heavily serrated blade set studded with thorns from the many ravenous plants of the Sulfurous Wetlands. The accursed magic used to mutate the native flora has been likewise imbued to these blades, enabling for a unique yet precise slashing technique.
A sickly piece of armor, grown from the twisted bark from one of the few remaining Ent Ancients that still populate the Sulfurous Wetlands.
This necklace once signified the everlasting bond between two people. As if alive, this necklace carries with it an aura of spite and anguish.
A live spiderling with a string formed of its own silk, a druid may befriend it to gain increased familiarity with the form of a spider, a traditionally hard form to undertake.
A well-crafted bow made from the hardened spider exoskeletons and their powerful silk.
Stuck in the maw of the Son of Arachna, this Katana is coated in the creature’s poisonous venom. In the hands of the right person, this is a truly deadly weapon.

This formerly ordinary mace was twisted by Arachna’s son after plucking it from a hapless victim. Though he has no reason to use it himself, some believe the Son of Arachna keeps it as reminder to his kin to not cross him as he did his mother.
Chitin armor fashioned from the exoskeleton of a blue Beehemoth. Ultra-light and extremely flexible, yet surprisingly tough.
Chitin armor fashioned from the exoskeleton of a red Beehemoth. Ultra-light and extremely flexible, yet surprisingly tough.
Chitin armor fashioned from the exoskeleton of a yellow Beehemoth. Ultra-light and extremely flexible, yet surprisingly tough.
The blade of this dagger was once the enormous Killer Bee Queen’s stinger. It remains exceptionally sharp, and could easily cut through even the heaviest of armor in a single strike.
Shaped from the stingers of mutant beehemoths, these blades are sealed away until the very moment of combat and brandished by the finest of shinobee.
Fashioned by the Beekeeper, this pendant allows the wearer to command the forces of the nest directing swarms of bees to attack the wearer’s enemies.
A nefarious helm combining pheromones and Killer Honey to captivate Killer Bees into servitude.
The guiding power of the realm’s pollenating drones. Even when stolen from their proper queen, it can still briefly influence their minds to service.
Equipped with a mechanical combustion technology few may understand, this weapon projects flames boasting temperatures capable of melting their target before they’re able to retaliate.
A flexible ironwax bow created by the Felwasp Guards to defend their larvae from assailants.
A strange trap that subdues would-be pilferers with a torrent of sticky honey.
Lightweight, yet protective armor taken from the shed exoskeleton of a warrior bee.
The solid honey on this ring renews its wearer with incredible energy.
Chitin armor fashioned from the exoskeleton of a Green Beehemoth. Ultra-light and extremely flexible, yet surprisingly tough.
A quiver crafted from the remains of a green Beehemoth, filled with razor-sharp arrows fashioned from Killer Bee stingers.
Armor of the bridge sentinel, adorned with honors which now serve only as a bitter reminder of what once was.
Exceedingly large and challenging to handle, this titanic blade embodies the stoic might of its true owner.
A dreadfully heavy barbute, prominently featuring inward curving horns. Such a trait has been considered taboo for eons, further marking the age of this design.
Use of magical weapons is typically reserved for the king’s mage units. For a swordsman to receive such a fine piece of craft was considered a great honor, and perhaps still is.

A colossal gauntlet of masterful composition. Its sheer size is disproportionate for most warriors, only able to be worn on one’s forearm as a bracer. A once glossy exterior faded from battle long ago, yet the sturdiness of its make is as resolute as it ever was.
Formerly a nobleman’s uniform, now workshopped by the court magician into a gloomy coat befitting of his accursed company.
An untempered casting wand brought forth to tap into distant powers, but an imbalance rendered it too unreliable for most to dare use.
This deeply profaned magitech haunts those who bear it with distant, unending screams. One’s conscience must be far beyond all reason to use its tremendous and varied powers without vacillation.
A perfectly concentrated blend of elemental forces, primed to diverge into an explosive result if the seal is ever broken. This flawless balance is believed to be the key to a still greater power.

A peculiar stone, tremendously heavy for its size and seemingly not of this world. Its very presence in the realm raises a great deal more questions than answers, yet the uncanny energy it emanates provides only one haunting clue.
The lavish robes of the kingdom’s royalty, outfitted with pauldrons on both shoulders for a more distinguished appearance. A sinister presence remains channeled through the draping fabric.

Rarely brandished and in pristine condition, the old monarchy deemed this exquisite recurve too dignified for all but royalty to handle. Though it remains unproven in practical combat, this bow’s raw potential is unmatched, waiting to be drawn out through pure focus.

A veritable artifact from the forgotten splendor of a kingdom none wish to remember. No matter the craftsmanship of its gilded design, this unwelcome sight is now only a sobering reminder of a mistake without regret.
Endless melodies from this fine instrument once filled the halls of the castle. It is without any apparent wear, yet even the most upbeat tune sounds somber when it is played.
An eerie reminder of what lays in the depths of the Shatters.
Part of a sacramental rite, the memory of which has faded with those who observed its divine grace.

As the nameless monarch sat malcontent upon the throne, his ambitions and impiety became one, and with this came enlightenment. A legacy reduced to less than legend, utterly erased from the nescient minds of the land, with its only epitaph being the sorry remnant of his majesty’s dearest. After all, what good are kings in a realm of gods?

This transmogrified crook bolsters the ability to peer into pocket dimensions and cling to the essence within. Such a characteristic arguably makes the staff itself sentient, a harrowing trait for the tool which gave the Shattered Kingdom its name.

A cryptic scroll from the castle archives, unlike anything else in the forsaken collection. Only with supreme occult power may one harness the equalized power of the immortal elements, a technique long attributed to mere legend.
On simple observation, this robe appears unremarkably standard for the dressings of the court magician. But perhaps this attribute is all too emblematic of kingdom’s folly.
An unsettling glow looms beneath the partitioned gem’s surface, ever-changing yet always in immaculate balance. Soon enough its power may be unleashed again.
The eldest of Daeva warriors crafted this sacred weapon to smite all that dare oppose the forces of the light.
A glorious artifact, bestowed upon mortals from Daeva warriors. Emulating their altruistic goals, this prism can forge countless fighters from the pure lights of the heavens.
These garments have been meticulously crafted over the millennia to serve the Daeva, helping them ward off and fight against the ever burning forces of darkness.
A mere flame from the legendary forgefire used by Daeva master crafters, this trinket grants awe-inspiring powers to those deemed worthy.
This cryptic blade has been forged from beligerent energies of the great beyond. Wielding it twists mortal comprehension of right and wrong.
A mystifying sigil from an otherwordly domain. The power sealed within causes it to faintly pull at its surroundings, attempting to draw them into the void.

The individual plates of this armor shift constantly in and out of realms under the Void’s influence. The few mortals that have found, let alone are able to wear this cursed armor become entranced in protective and destructive forces.
Though lacking physical form, this trinket grants a sense of sight that allows beings from the realm of shadow to peer into the worlds bathed in light.
A famous boxer once said the secret to their victory was having a great right hook, this dagger’s craftsman took that literally.
This stylish felt cap has found itself at the helm of many tavern brawls, and atop the heads of many poor decision makers.
A finely crafted leather bound quiver containing many small darts. The reduced size of the arrows in this quiver allow a specific firing pattern, confusing their target as if they were seeing double.
A cobbled together suit of armor made from a beer barrel, while lacking in real defensive properties, its alcoholic aura numbs the wearer’s pain against attacks.
A small golden band meant to be worn on one’s ear. This mundane looking accessory appears to give the wearer an increased tolerance to intoxicants.
An angular wakizashi crafted from the core of the Shadow Cube God. In spite of the narrow blade, it can unleash quite a devastating onslaught.
A bulky cloak ill-suited for stealth, but can provide the wearer with great offensive capabilities.
A seal granting the user access to other dimensions, though the means by which this is accomplished are unknown.
A radiant staff with a square-shaped conduit at its edge. Only the most prestigious of cuboid monarchs are allowed to wield such a weapon.
The angular remains of a cube. It can be temporarily resurrected through the dark arts.
These garbs aren’t fitted for humans, making them quite uncomfortable. However, the protective properties it bestows upon the wearer are invaluable.
Fitted with a cube shaped crystal, this band is capable of mitigating harm to the wearer.
The dolomite stone makes you feel able to stand against anything.
The malachite stone makes you feel at peace with the land.
The azure stone makes you feel ready for any situation.
A tome dedicated to the works of a forgotten god. It is written in a strange script that glows with a pure white light.
An unusually crafted lute with an ancient style of construction, meant to produce soothing sounds of deep sleep and keep wrathful gods dormant.
Despite their normal pacificity, priests of Geb were able to protect themselves from aggressors.
Within, the priests of the past have catalogued the story and knowledge of Geb.
A ceremonial garment worn by those who worship the god Geb.
It is said that the ring attunes its wearer to the earth and its life force.
An astronomically heavy katana. It is forged from dark matter.
A deadly substance concocted with ingredients found in the sewers.
An appalling dagger with a blade forged of a highly resilient metal, one of the very few materials that can withstand the corrosive properties surrounding it.
Exposure to this ferocious concoction is so intensely lethal that it acts instantaneously. Its deceptively small vial allows for easy use.
A specially coated armor designed with the intent of nullifying illnesses. It is most beneficial when used in tandem with equipment of similar attributes.
A cursed gemstone imbued with numerous plagues. While unfavorable to one’s stamina, it proves itself useful with the associated resistances.
No mortal can fire this dreaded bow without resting in between shots. It requires tremendous skill to wield.
This ghastly blade has the power to emit waves of energy that dance like wraiths in the dark.

Shaping raw magic into spirit-like forms, phantasmal projectiles are launched, each one homing in on the target
with the precision of a well trained archer.
A ghastly ring that calls upon the eager hands of an impossible undead creature.
My staff led me to a strange arena, which seemed to have a dark aura about it.
His gift was gracious, but I would not dabble in spirits. He challenged me to a duel.
I found a great spectre floating above me. He reached out and granted me his cranium.
Our fight was glorious, but in the end, I fell in battle and joined him in undeath.
A strange ectoplasmic concoction that can inflict its blight on living and etheral alike.
A bow of light forged by the angels for use in their eternal struggle against darkness and evil.
Fashioned from the finest logs found within the Woodland Labyrinths, this bow uses sharp leaves instead of conventional arrows. Such lightweight ammunition allows for quick movement.
Carved from the heartwood of an ancient tree and imbued with the whispering spirits of the forest, this staff channels centuries of wisdom.
A wakizashi used by a powerful swordsman in ancient times. It seems to dance through the air as you swing it.

The Woodland Labyrinth is a lamentable example of mortal meddling in the ways of nature, a harsh reality its occupants are keenly aware of. A sheath like this, fully embracing their own ways of life, is no doubt a bitter expression of dissent.
A lavish golden ring adorned with a prized ruby from Dreadstump’s spoils.
A prickly bow with unshaven thorns jutting out from the grip, making it unwieldy to fire.
A silky case of arrows woven by Mama Megamoth’s larvae for use by her woodland warriors.
A dagger fashioned from the fang of an enormous spider.
An arachnid star which slings silk to tangle predators while granting oneself the speed and stamina of Arachna’s most agile young.
A ring crafted from the faceted eye of an enormous spider.
An ancient staff used both for sacrifice and communion with the gods.
A relic uncovered from the depths of the jungle. It exudes a deep darkness.
A ceremonial robe once worn by the Tlatoani.
A ring made from a splinter of crystallized sacrificial bone.
A magical honeycomb that was once crucial in repelling an assault from the Hornet Rebellion.
Stolen from the Queen Bee herself, this scepter possesses the power to destroy evil.
An extraordinarily dense silversteel anchor attached to a magical kelpwoven chain. Whatever ship it was attached to has long since been dragged to the depths.
A quiver crafted from the remains of a yellow Beehemoth, filled with razor-sharp arrows fashioned from Killer Bee stingers.
A quiver crafted from the remains of a red Beehemoth, filled with razor-sharp arrows fashioned from Killer Bee stingers.
A quiver crafted from the remains of a blue Beehemoth, filled with razor-sharp arrows fashioned from Killer Bee stingers.
Many Rogues have tried to use this to escape their asylum. All have failed.
Even in small amounts, this potent venom is capable of corroding the armor of its victims.
This legendary dagger was commissioned by a band of mad rogues to prove the worth of the Dirk.
This longbow has protected the mountain shrines for centuries in the hands of countless legendary archers. The hail of arrows it fires is said to purify wretched spirits of corruption.
This helm was forged of metal that fell from the sky. It is strangely light and emits a faint hum.
A weapon used by Skuld’s soul collectors to absorb and send souls to their final destinations.
Imbued with haunting energies of a foreign rite, the layered blade of this shadowy tool is never finished with its victim.
This orb of ancient bloodstone has a dark reputation of twisting those who wield it.
This strange quiver was created in a forgotten age by a lost tribe of Dark Elves.
A great katana charged with the power of the sun itself.
A seal that is said to briefly borrow the power of the Gods themselves. Some Paladins shun its dark power, others embrace it.
This massive shield was created for the wealthy and powerful Ogre kings as a sign of their unrivaled strength.
Thought to have been lost to the ages, the wisdom imparted in this tablet will summon a power unrivaled by any modern spell.
An aged coin of unfamiliar design, gently glowing with the spirits of warmth. This seemingly modest keepsake puts your mind at ease, mending your wounds.
An aged coin of unfamiliar design, gently glowing with the spirits of happiness. This seemingly modest keepsake invigorates you body, readying you for the next move.
A long lost artifact recovered by Davy Jones, believed to have been cursed by the gods of the seven seas themselves.
A mighty mug for a mighty drink! Be merry! Sprite Credits: Dappertron
A barrel filled to the brim with the cheapest ale money can buy. The cask is highly pressurized and must be handled with care, should it be dropped it is likely to explode violently.
A necessary component for any beer-drinking or sausage-eating in the month of October. Sprite Credits: Dappertron
A lightweight blade that seems to dance in your hands.
A sound almost like a wind chime emanates softly from this wand.
A faithful and comfortable scarf that has witnessed countless journeys. Imbued with a protective enchantment, it allows its wearer to gracefully weave through perilous situations.

A majestic trident adorned with enchanted coral formations that enables command of the tides. Once wielded by King Alexander in his conquest of the oceans, its power has since faded with each passing moon from natural weathering.

A wondrous bow of heavenly origin, harnessing Daevic magic to bend and concentrate light into tangible projectiles. At the point where these beams intersect, a dazzling burst of celestial brilliance radiates.
A sinister twist on the scarecrow’s intended use. Totalia bewitched these cursed figures with an uncanny allure to enthral victims into their untimely demise.
Although cannons were once typically associated with naval warfare, many pirates now regard Bilgewater’s application of them in land-based combat as revolutionary.

An unnaturally pristine skull adorned with a crown of thorns. Various parts of this once holy figure continue to be worshipped as sacred relics post-mortem as they bestow graces and miracles through the traces of divine power abiding within.

An accursed sigil that beckons to one’s inner demons. Unholy paladins can unleash their dormant latent potential by forming a pact with an otherworldly force through a tribute of their own flesh and blood.

A resplendent, gilded lance once wielded by a legendary horseback rider of yore during the great Draconic Cataclysm. Its formidable reach could slay monstrous dragons with seemingly impenetrable bodies by impaling their deeply buried hearts.
An enchanting cloak that conceals its wearer in a veil of iridescent bubbles. The shimmering cascade of bubbles serves as the only lifeline within the sea’s deepest hadal trenches.

An ancient volume recording ancestral lineages dating as far back as the Primal Era. Infused with the essence of druidic magic, it grants the power to delve into nature’s eternal transformation, morphing its caster into formidable creatures.

A diabolical fusion of magic and chemical warfare engineered by Dr. Terrible in his effort to subjugate the Nest. When unleashed, it triggers a cataclysmic chain reaction between the charged volatile pollen that engulfs the air in a blinding and roaring inferno.

A malevolent katana infested with parasitic organisms. The ravenous symbiotes instills in its user a thirst for blood, which grows as it drinks from the life force of its wielder and their foes alike.

A stone slab adorned with engimatic symbols, harkening to an era of ancient technology still shrouded  in obscurity. These same inscrutable runes are found etched on surfaces within the Tundra, beckoning those who dare to decipher their secrets.
Suitable for practice.
A blade made from iron stuck in firm dark oak. The raw power of each strike compensates for the blunt edge.
A heavy iron blade set in a handle crafted from tough wood from the eastern tundras. Each slash carries immense amounts of force.
A breastplate crafted from sturdy plantlife and shards of iron. The plants seem to react when hit heavily.
A handy compass that reminds travelers of the homes they will someday return to.
I was a fool before, but now everything is so clear. The phylactery speaks to me, knows me, protects me.
All shall know my power. Not even the Mad God can stop me.
…finally, I cornered and killed the beast. When I awoke in a cold sweat…I felt youthful, powerful.
As I lifted the stone from its resting place it became readily apparent there was more to it than met the eye…
Displaying this malformed mango in public is said to awaken the rage of a long-dormant entity from beyond.
Crispy, buttered, wheat or rye. In toast it lives, in toast it dies.
A highly unstable staff engineered to expel forceful bolts of an energy component known as K.I.D.D. (Knee-deep In Dungeon Design). It’s not great now, but allegedly the second version will be better.
The harbinger of past pandemonium, current craziness, and future fatuity. It just can’t seem to stay focused.
A heavy mace that abides only by the power of repetition.
A katana once belonging to a royal sorcerer known for great ambition, but slow delivery. His treasured blade was prophesied to produce something wonderful… eventually.

After years of tinkering with the algorithms, Null created a deadlier replica of Mister Mango to create the Groovy Mango. This jamming mango was given the time to sweeten and ripen into a groovy party rocking entity.
After years of tinkering with the algorithms, Null created a deadlier replica of the Butter Bow to create the Baked Bow. This bow showcases the power of butter, enjoy these tasty baked treats.

After years of tinkering with the algorithms, Null created a deadlier replica of the Caduceus of Current Craziness to create the Caduceus Of Enlightened Chaos. Using this wand allows the user to reach their full chaotic potential and understand their role in battle; be unpredictable and let someone else deal with it.

After years of tinkering with the algorithms, Null created a deadlier replica of the K.I.D.D. Force to create the K.I.D.D. Force 2.0. Upgraded with even less shades, this staff has really come around.

After years of tinkering with the algorithms, Null created a deadlier replica of the MMace MMurderer to create the FFabulous FFlail. A MMace that can be MMasterfully FFlung, FFrequently FFlying FFabulously albeit with slight miscalculations

After years of tinkering with the algorithms, Null created a deadlier replica of the Blade of Ages to create the Blade of Blooming. Nature would slowly takeover this blade blooming with beautiful roses.
A brilliant cloak that utilizes the complex process of light reflection to perfectly conceal its user by creating an exact replica, mimicking the wearer.
A quiver once treasured by a highly sentimental archer who just couldn’t bear to part with her arrows forever. They’ve become rather dull after so much recycling.
An instructional spell used as an education tool for the training of young wizards. Before they run, they must learn to talk.

The deepest wounds are not physical, but psychological. Only the wisest healers are savvy enough to apply the advanced treatment this tome enables, guaranteed to revitalize allies in their times of need.

It’s not easy being the best. Some wish for more, a way to inflict difficulties on themselves so that they may once again experience what it’s like to not be insultingly overpowered. At last, there is a way.
As the heavy armor worn by most knights typically conceals their faces with a stoic expression, they require other ways to convey emotions of shock or excitement.

The fruit of over a decade of research, this blessed seal at long last overcomes all limitations on its usage and never allows its user to run out of strength. A slight concession had to be made by devoting one hundred percent of its magic resources back into magic.

A misguided attempt by Xolotl to grant a form of his power to followers among the Secluded Thicket, with the glaring weakness that it can only be utilized in large, open areas with direct access to the clouds.
The skull of an enlightened philosopher who understood the true potential of necromancers to be summoners. Tragically, his overwhelmingly high IQ got the best of him.
A devastating trap with some major hang time. It’s full of hot air and not actually that useful, which is surely a metaphor for something.
What goes around comes around.
All that glitters is not gold. Beware, tricksters who choose this dastardly prism must be committed to their cruel prank.
After years of the truth being rejected by the masses, this version of the fabled Honey Scepter is automatically enhanced with the full potential it had all along.
A star with high potential, but curses the user with a crippling lack of decisiveness.

Formerly a nobleman’s uniform, now workshopped by the court magician into a gloomy coat befitting of his accursed company.

Rarely brandished and in pristine condition, the old monarchy deemed this exquisite recurve too dignified for all but royalty to handle. Though it remains unproven in practical combat, this bow’s raw potential is unmatched, waiting to be drawn out through pure focus.

A veritable artifact from the forgotten splendor of a kingdom none wish to remember. No matter the craftsmanship of its gilded design, this unwelcome sight is now only a sobering reminder of a mistake without regret.
Endless melodies from this fine instrument once filled the halls of the castle. It is without any apparent wear, yet even the most upbeat tune sounds somber when it is played.
An eerie reminder of what lays in the depths of the Shatters.

A peculiar stone, tremendously heavy for its size and seemingly not of this world. Its very presence in the realm raises a great deal more questions than answers, yet the uncanny energy it emanates provides only one haunting clue.
A perfectly concentrated blend of elemental forces, primed to diverge into an explosive result if the seal is ever broken. This flawless balance is believed to be the key to a still greater power.
